Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL BAKONDI, TEL. NO. 450-0424 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendants The City And County Of San Francisco and The San Francisco Police Department's Motion To Strike 2nd Amended Complaint is Denied. Whether the 10B Program violates the City Charter 4.127 and Police Code 1750 may ultimately be questions of law, but the resolution may require a factual record beyond each parties' request for judicial notice. Further, much of the motion seeks to strike portions of the pleading with too fine a point at the pleading stage (e.g., page 3, line 15-16; para 11, line 5 (striking only the phrase "the Municipal Police Code"), para. 13, line 14-15 (striking only the phrase "the San Francisco Charter, Police Code")) or unnecessarily (e.g., paragraphs 9, 10). Pleadings are to be construed liberally, with a view toward substantial justice. See CCP 452. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Defendant The City And County Of San Francisco, A Public, The San Francisco Police Department, A Public Demurrer To 2nd Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-10-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. DANIEL BAKONDI, TEL. NO. 450-0424 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. WAYNE K. SNODGRASS, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4675 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; Defendants The City And County Of San Francisco and The San Francisco Police Department's Demurrer To First, Third and Fourth Causes of action in 2nd Amended Complaint is Sustained, without leave to amend, as to Defendant CCSF and SFPD's demurrer to the first cause of action, and overruled as to the third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action, a government entity is not considered a person under Bus. & Prof. 17200, and thus cannot be sued. (People for the Ethical Treatment of Animals, Inc. v. California Milk Producers Advisory Bd. (2005) 125 Cal.App.4th 871, 879; Trinkle v. California State Lottery (1999) 71 Cal.App.4th 1198, 1202; 13 Witkin, Cal. Summary (10th ed. 2005) Equity, 124, p. 438.) As to the third and fourth causes of action, Plaintiffs have alleged a real property interest in their "beats." at paragraph 138 of the Second Amended Complaint ("Plaintiffs have a San Francisco Charter-conferred and constitutionally protected real property right and real property in their patrol beats"), which is incorporated by reference in paragraphs 115 (third cause of action) and 128 (fourth cause of action). For purposes of demurrer, the allegations of the complaint are taken as true. Order signed in open court. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185
